title: Add JSON import/export mode for Job Template and Run submission forms
body:
## Summary
Job Template and Run submission require manual line-by-line entry in textareas, which is slow and error-prone for real operations.

## Why this matters
- Advanced users already have payloads/scripts and want to paste structured config.
- Manual key=value and arg line editing causes typos and inconsistent runs.
- Replaying known-good runs should be deterministic and fast.

## Proposal
Add JSON import/export and JSON editing mode to both:
- Create Job Template
- Submit Run

## Functional Requirements
- Toggle between "Form" and "JSON" modes.
- JSON schema validation with actionable error messages.
- Round-trip fidelity (Form -> JSON -> Form with no data loss).
- Support copy/export of submitted payload for reruns.
- Keep existing RBAC and idempotency behavior unchanged.

## Example JSON (job template)
```json
{
  "name": "demo-job-4",
  "artifact_uri": "s3://.../sparkpilot_demo_job.py",
  "artifact_digest": "sha256:placeholder",
  "entrypoint": "main",
  "args": ["s3://.../input/events.json", "s3://.../output/demo-job-4-<timestamp>/"],
  "spark_conf": {
    "spark.executor.instances": "1",
    "spark.executor.memory": "1g",
    "spark.driver.memory": "1g"
  },
  "retry_max_attempts": 1,
  "timeout_seconds": 1200
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Users can submit valid job/run definitions entirely from JSON.
- Invalid JSON shows clear field-level/line-level errors.
- Existing form UX remains available and stable.

title: UI is too unintuitive for demos; add in-product guided setup + JSON mode for job/run forms
body:
## Summary
The Runs/Job Template UX is currently too unintuitive for live demos. Critical setup decisions are hidden in docs/tribal memory, so users can spend hours iterating with failed runs before finding known-good values.

## Problem
- Markdown-only guidance is not enough during live use.
- Users need in-product guidance at the point of action (create template / submit run).
- Current form experience is tedious and error-prone for repeated runs (manual key/value entry).

## What happened in real testing (March 17, 2026)
- Repeated confusion around required fields and what exact values worked.
- Repeated failed runs from invalid/missing args and wrong bucket paths.
- Capacity-related run stalls were not clearly distinguished from normal running.
- Hard to confidently reproduce a successful run in front of someone.

## Requested Product Changes
### 1) In-UI guided setup (not docs-only)
Add a "Demo-safe" / "Known-good profile" panel directly in the Job Template + Run Submit UI:
- pre-submit checklist (environment ready, preflight ready, input artifact exists, capacity sanity)
- known-good example values
- inline validation hints/remediation next to fields
- one-click copy/apply example profile

### 2) JSON mode for form entry
Allow users to paste/edit full template/run payloads as JSON, with round-trip to form fields:
- toggle: Form mode <-> JSON mode
- validate and show line-level parse errors
- import/export JSON payload
- preserve idempotency with deterministic payload previews

### 3) Safer defaults + warnings
- warn when output prefix reused
- warn when args count likely mismatches entrypoint contract
- warn when requested executor shape is likely to stall on current cluster

## Acceptance Criteria
- A first-time user can create and run a known-good demo without leaving the UI.
- Template/run can be fully authored in JSON and submitted successfully.
- UI explains "what to put where" inline for every required field.
- Demo flow can be repeated with <10 minutes setup and no hidden steps.

title: Cost page lacks actual-cost reconciliation status (estimated vs CUR pending vs reconciled)
body:
## Summary
Cost UI does not clearly explain when actual CUR cost is pending vs reconciled.

## Impact
Users think cost tracking is broken when actual is delayed.

## Expected
- Per-run status: Estimated only / CUR pending / Reconciled
- Reconciliation health and last-success timestamps

## Proposed Fix
- Add per-run cost status badge
- Show CUR worker health and reconciliation timestamps
- Warn when CUR config is missing/incomplete

title: Run queue/capacity blocking is opaque when multiple runs are submitted
body:
## Summary
When multiple runs are submitted, users cannot tell queued/capacity-blocked vs truly running.

## Impact
Perceived hangs and poor confidence in scheduler behavior.

## Evidence
- Repeated logs: "Initial job has not accepted any resources"
- Pending executor pods while run status remains running/accepted

## Expected
- Explicit states: queued, waiting_for_capacity, dispatching, running
- Show queue position and blocking reason

## Proposed Fix
- Add capacity-blocked reason surfaces (insufficient nodes, pending executors, autoscaling in progress)
- Show pending duration and queue metadata in Runs UI

title: Runs log viewer is not reliable live tail (appears broken)
body:
## Summary
Run logs in the Runs page can appear stuck or empty while EMR job is active/successful.

## Impact
Operators cannot trust in-product logs and must pivot to AWS console/CLI.

## Evidence
- Run: cf648f3d-bbae-4c17-8f17-300bdf849dae
- EMR Job Run: 0000000377sp1oka3cf
- UI showed only JAVA_TOOL_OPTIONS lines while run completed and output landed in S3.

## Expected
- Live tail for active runs
- Recent tail for completed runs
- Clear loading/empty/error states

## Proposed Fix
- Backend: paginate filter_log_events and return latest N lines
- Frontend: auto-refresh selected run logs each poll interval
- UX: live-tail indicator + explicit fetch error state

title: Runs appear blocked when another run is active; queue and capacity reason not visible
body:
Summary:
When one run is active or cluster capacity is constrained, additional runs appear not to start. Users cannot tell if this is EMR queueing, scheduler behavior, or Kubernetes resource starvation.

Repro date: March 17, 2026
Environment: us-east-1 / sparkpilot-demo-2 (4c7d381a-676c-4d1a-921f-ec05144db73b)
Observed runs:
- demo-job-3 run db53ac19-d9dc-46af-9c00-17829fc780a2
- demo-job-4 run 0000000377sp1oka3cf (EMR state SUBMITTED while another run was active)

Observed behavior:
- Runs stay in accepted/submitted/running with no clear blocking reason in UI.
- Logs/events indicate insufficient cpu and executor starvation, but this is not surfaced as a primary reason.

Expected behavior:
- UI should show explicit reason per run: waiting for capacity, waiting in queue, or dispatch blocked.
- UI should show per-environment concurrency and current in-flight occupancy.

Proposed fix:
- Add scheduler/queue reason fields to API and display in Runs UI.
- Surface Kubernetes FailedScheduling and Insufficient cpu as first-class diagnostics.
- Add tests for multi-run submission under constrained capacity

title: [UI/API] Costs selector semantics mismatch: 'Team' UI uses tenant IDs, not Team entities
body:
?Problem
- The Costs page label says "Team", but the selector is populated from `environments[].tenant_id` (derived from environment list), not from `/v1/teams`.
- Access page can show "No teams yet" while Costs still shows multiple "teams", which is misleading.

Current behavior evidence
- `ui/app/costs/page.tsx`:
  - Initializes selected value with `rows[0].tenant_id`
  - Builds options from unique `environments.map((item) => item.tenant_id)`
- Backend cost endpoints currently use `team` query parameter as tenant key semantics.

Impact
- Operator confusion during real-AWS validation and governance setup.
- Incorrect mental model for RBAC/governance: users expect Costs team selector to reflect Team entities managed in Access.

Requested fix
- Align terminology and data model between Costs and Access.
- Option A: Rename selector to "Tenant" everywhere in Costs + API docs.
- Option B: Use real Team entities for selector (`/v1/teams`) and map showback by team_id.
- Add explicit UX copy for whichever model is chosen.

Acceptance Criteria
- Costs selector source and label are consistent with backend semantics.
- Access and Costs no longer present contradictory "team" state.
- Add regression test(s) proving selector data source and label semantics.
- Update docs/runbook with final model (tenant-key or team-key).

Related
- #33 (CUR chargeback)
- #81 (Access page UX)

title: [Auth/UI] Handle mock OIDC key-rotation token invalidation with clear recovery UX
body:
?Problem
- In local/live-dev stack, OIDC container restart rotates signing keys and invalidates existing UI token before expiry.
- Users see confusing "Signature verification failed" even when token lifetime remains.

Acceptance Criteria
- Detect signature-failure due to key rotation and prompt explicit token refresh in UI.
- Consider stable dev key option for mock OIDC to reduce churn across container restarts.
- Update docs/scripts to clarify when token must be refreshed.
- Add regression test for stale-token-after-oidc-restart behavior.

Notes
- Complements #69 and #75; dev UX hardening.

title: [UI] Run submit layout polish: preflight status spacing and action-group readability
body:
Problem
- Run submit section has tight spacing around preflight status and action buttons, causing visual crowding.

Acceptance Criteria
- Add consistent spacing hierarchy between form fields, action buttons, preflight badge, and check list.
- Ensure focus/hover states remain accessible after layout tweaks.
- Validate no regressions on mobile and narrow widths.
- Add a small visual regression test/screenshot baseline for this block.

Notes
- Cosmetic but high-frequency UX pain in live usage.

title: [UI] Job template editor UX: multiline args/spark_conf ergonomics and validation
body:
Problem
- `Args (one per line)` and `Spark Conf (key=value per line)` textareas are hard to scan/edit and feel visually broken.

Acceptance Criteria
- Improve textarea sizing, spacing, and typography for multiline editing.
- Add helper examples/placeholders for valid formats.
- Add inline parse feedback with line-level messaging where feasible.
- Ensure mobile/desktop responsive layout remains clean.
- Add UI tests for parse/validation messages.

Notes
- Relates to #23 (UI job-create flow).

title: [UI] Access page usability overhaul: guided admin workflow + clearer validation
body:
Problem
- Access page has high cognitive load and unclear operational path (token, identities, teams, scopes).
- New operators are unsure what sequence to follow.

Acceptance Criteria
- Add concise guided workflow section on Access page (token -> identity -> team -> scope).
- Improve labels/help text for each section and expected inputs.
- Add inline validation and clearer error mapping for common auth/bootstrap failures.
- Preserve existing API contracts.
- Add tests for guided states and validation messages.

Notes
- Related to #75 (production IdP login + subject mapping).

title: [UI] Run Diagnostics UX: explicit run selector and selected-run context
body:
Problem
- Run Diagnostics panel relies on row-level `Diag` action with weak guidance.
- Users struggle to understand which run is selected for diagnostics.

Acceptance Criteria
- Add explicit diagnostics run selector/state indicator in Runs page.
- Show currently selected run id/job/status near diagnostics panel header.
- Keep row-level action as shortcut but sync with selector state.
- Improve empty/loading/error states with actionable text.
- Add UI tests for selector sync and diagnostics fetch behavior.

Notes
- Relates to runs UX hardening from live testing.

title: [UI] Run submission guardrail: only allow selectable ready environments
body:
Problem
- Run submission UI allows selecting environments that are not in `ready` state.
- This leads to avoidable failed preflight and noisy operator workflow.

Acceptance Criteria
- Run submit environment dropdown only shows `ready` environments by default.
- If non-ready environments are optionally visible, they must be disabled and labeled with status.
- Job dropdown must only show jobs belonging to selectable ready environments.
- Submit action blocked with clear message when selected environment is not ready.
- Add tests for filtering/disable behavior and submit guardrails.

Notes
- Directly requested from real-AWS testing feedback.

title: [UI] Environment create flow: live provisioning progress + auto-refresh status
body:
Problem
- Environment creation currently requires manual refresh/navigation to observe terminal status.
- Users cannot see provisioning progress in real time from the create flow.

Acceptance Criteria
- After create, auto-poll operation/environment status with bounded interval/timeout.
- Display queued/in-progress/ready/failed states inline without page reload.
- Surface failure reason and remediation snippet when provisioning fails.
- Preserve existing API wiring; no contract changes required.
- Add tests for polling state transitions and failure rendering.

Notes
- Relates to #22 and #37.

title: [UI] Environment lifecycle controls: retry failed provisioning + delete environment
body:
Problem
- Failed environments cannot be retried from UI after fixing IAM/OIDC/preflight prerequisites.
- No delete action exists for failed or stale environments, which creates clutter and operator confusion.

Why this matters
- Real-AWS validation loops require iterative fix-and-retry.
- Orphaned failed environments make run selection and diagnostics error-prone.

Acceptance Criteria
- Add UI action to retry provisioning for an existing failed environment.
- Add UI action to delete environment (with confirmation and guardrails).
- Show clear success/error state for both actions in list/detail views.
- Block delete when environment has active/in-flight runs; return actionable message.
- Add API integration tests and UI tests for retry/delete behavior.

Notes
- Relates to #22 (UI env-create flow) and #37 (E2E env).

title: [Ops] Validate runtime IAM credential chain (task role -> AssumeRole customer role)

labels.name: status:needs-live-aws-evidence
labels.description: Do not close until explicit live-AWS proof is linked

body:
Problem
- Production security model depends on control-plane runtime credentials (ECS task role or equivalent) assuming customer roles via STS.
- Current validation focus is broad real-AWS flow; this specific credential-source and assume-role chain proof must be explicit.

Scope
- Validate runtime identity path on non-prod control plane deployment with NO static AWS keys in app containers.
- Prove API + provisioner + scheduler + reconciler obtain AWS credentials from runtime IAM role.
- Prove cross-account/customer-role assumption succeeds with expected `ExternalId` constraints.
- Capture failure/remediation for broken trust policy, wrong external ID, and denied `sts:AssumeRole`.

Acceptance Criteria
- Evidence shows `sts:GetCallerIdentity` from each runtime service resolves to control-plane task role principal (or equivalent runtime IAM principal).
- Evidence shows successful `AssumeRole` into customer role and subsequent EMR/EKS calls for BYOC-Lite flow.
- CloudTrail-linked evidence includes principal ARN, assumed role ARN, and request IDs for key calls.
- Confirm no long-lived static AWS creds are configured for production runtime services.

Related
- Parent deployment baseline: #15
- End-to-end environment: #37
- Second-operator pilot: #65

Definition of Done
- Keep issue open until explicit non-prod live-AWS evidence is attached.

title: [Auth] Production IdP login + subject mapping (remove manual token as default)

labels.name: status:needs-live-aws-evidence
labels.description: Do not close until explicit live-AWS proof is linked

body:
Problem
- Current local-dev flow relies on manual bearer-token paste and explicit identity bootstrap, which is not acceptable as the primary production user experience.

Scope
- Implement production login path in UI via external IdP (Auth0/Okta/Cognito) using OIDC Authorization Code + PKCE.
- Treat manual token paste as dev-only fallback (feature-flag or clearly hidden in production).
- Add subject (`sub`) to user-identity mapping flow so authenticated users do not need to enter tenant UUIDs manually for normal operations.
- Document bootstrap/admin-only steps separately from normal user flow.

Acceptance Criteria
- Normal user can sign in via IdP and access authorized pages without pasting a token.
- Access page and API flows resolve permissions from mapped identity/claims.
- Manual token flow is disabled or clearly non-default in production builds.
- Evidence includes at least one non-prod external IdP login trace plus role-scoped API requests.

Related
- Validation matrix: #69
- CSP/auth hardening: #59, #58
- Parent governance: #35

Definition of Done
- Keep issue open until explicit non-prod live-IdP evidence is attached.

title: [Backend] Add Databricks-on-AWS dispatch backend

labels.name: status:needs-live-aws-evidence
labels.description: Do not close until explicit live-AWS proof is linked

body:
Problem

Scope

- Implement auth/session model and workspace preflight checks (permissions/cluster policy/pool readiness).
- Integrate lifecycle, cancellation, diagnostics mapping, and cost attribution hooks.
- Add API/UI engine support and docs for Databricks-on-AWS mode.

Acceptance Criteria
- End-to-end run submission/monitor/cancel works through SparkPilot API for Databricks backend.
- Backend-specific policy/preflight checks are enforceable and auditable.
- Evidence artifacts include Databricks run IDs + SparkPilot run IDs + cost mapping samples.

Related

Definition of Done

labels.name: status:needs-live-aws-evidence

Scope

title: [Backend] Add EMR Serverless dispatch backend

labels.name: status:needs-live-aws-evidence
labels.description: Do not close until explicit live-AWS proof is linked

body:
Problem
- Current dispatch backend is effectively EMR on EKS only.
- EMR Serverless expands AWS Spark TAM with lower customer K8s complexity.

Scope
- Add EMR Serverless execution backend behind engine abstraction.
- Implement backend-specific preflight profile.
- Wire lifecycle/reconciliation/logs/cost attribution for Serverless runs.
- Add API/UI support for engine selection and backend metadata.

Acceptance Criteria
- Environment/job/run lifecycle works end-to-end with EMR Serverless.
- Preflight checks are backend-appropriate and enforceable.
- Airflow/Dagster integrations operate unchanged through API.

Related

Definition of Done

title: [Metrics] Establish baseline workload-contract outcomes from real runs

labels.name: status:needs-live-aws-evidence
labels.description: Do not close until explicit live-AWS proof is linked

body:
Problem
- Product value claims are currently qualitative; no baseline KPI set from real operator usage.

Scope
- Define and capture initial workload-contract metrics from live runs:
  - preflight block/warn/pass rates
  - prevented waste estimate (blocked-cost estimate)
  - dispatch success rate and retry rate
  - queue-to-running latency and terminal outcome distribution
  - budget guardrail trigger frequency
- Publish metric definitions and dashboard/report snapshots.

Acceptance Criteria
- Baseline metric set is documented and reproducible.
- Metrics are derived from real runs with linked evidence artifacts.
- Gaps/unknowns are identified with follow-up issues.

Related
- Parents: #37 #33 #39

Definition of Done
- Keep issue open until explicit non-prod live-AWS evidence is attached.

title: [Docs] Publish tenant isolation boundaries and security assumptions

labels.name: status:needs-live-aws-evidence
labels.description: Do not close until explicit live-AWS proof is linked

body:
Problem
- Tenant/team isolation guarantees and non-guarantees are not explicitly documented for enterprise/security review.

Scope
- Publish explicit isolation boundary document covering:
  - logical RBAC boundaries vs infrastructure boundaries
  - namespace isolation limitations
  - network, IAM, and data access assumptions
  - threat model summary and residual risks
- Include recommended hardening patterns for stricter isolation.

Acceptance Criteria
- Document is clear on what SparkPilot enforces vs what customer infra must enforce.
- Security/compliance review checklist is included.
- Linked from setup/deployment docs.

Related
- Parent: #35
- Informs enterprise readiness in #15

Definition of Done
- Keep issue open until explicit non-prod live-AWS evidence is attached.

title: [Auth] OIDC interoperability matrix with real IdPs (Cognito/Auth0/Okta)

labels.name: status:needs-live-aws-evidence
labels.description: Do not close until explicit live-AWS proof is linked

body:
Problem
- OIDC flow has mostly been validated against local mock OIDC, not production IdPs.

Scope
- Validate end-to-end auth flow with real providers (Cognito/Auth0/Okta at minimum one, ideally all three).
- Confirm discovery, authorization code + PKCE, token validation, logout/session expiry behavior.
- Verify CSP/connect-src and issuer configuration in production-like deployment.

Acceptance Criteria
- Successful login and API authorization through at least one external IdP.
- Failure modes are documented with actionable remediation.
- Evidence includes issuer metadata URLs, token claims samples (redacted), and API access traces.

Related
- Parent: #35
- Related hardening: #59

Definition of Done

labels.name: status:needs-live-aws-evidence
labels.description: Do not close until explicit live-AWS proof is linked

Problem

- Specifically verify deferrable trigger execution and resume behavior under scheduler restarts.
- Validate auth/idempotency/retry behavior via real DAG runs.

Acceptance Criteria

- Deferrable trigger behavior is verified under normal and restart conditions.
- Evidence artifacts include DAG run IDs + SparkPilot run IDs + EMR JobRun IDs.

title: [FinOps] Validate CUR reconciliation against real CUR tables

labels.name: status:needs-live-aws-evidence
labels.description: Do not close until explicit live-AWS proof is linked

body:
Problem
- CUR reconciliation logic has only been verified against mocked Athena/CUR responses.
- FinOps value claim requires validation against real CUR tables and billing edge cases.

Scope
- Validate reconciliation against a real CUR dataset in Athena.
- Test edge cases: Savings Plans amortization, RI effects, Spot/On-Demand mix, delayed CUR partitions.
- Define acceptable variance thresholds and reconciliation windows.

Acceptance Criteria
- Real CUR reconciliation run produces expected actual/effective cost records.
- Variance thresholds are documented and met (or gaps tracked with follow-up issues).
- Evidence includes Athena query IDs, sample run IDs, and before/after allocation snapshots.

Related
- Parent: #33

title: [Validation] Real-AWS BYOC-Lite misconfiguration preflight matrix

labels.name: status:needs-live-aws-evidence
labels.description: Do not close until explicit live-AWS proof is linked

body:
Problem
- Preflight has mostly been validated on correctly configured environments.
- Core value proposition requires deterministic catches for real misconfigurations before spend.

Scope
- Run a real-AWS BYOC-Lite misconfiguration matrix and verify fail behavior for:
  - missing/incorrect execution-role trust
  - missing iam:PassRole
  - missing OIDC association
  - namespace collision/invalid namespace
  - release/runtime incompatibility scenarios where applicable
- Validate remediation text quality and audit events for each failure.

Acceptance Criteria
- Each scenario fails pre-dispatch with deterministic check codes and actionable remediation.
- No scenario reaches StartJobRun when hard-fail preflight criteria are unmet.
- Evidence artifacts include operation_id/environment_id/run_id where applicable.

Related
- Parent: #37
- Depends on existing check implementations in #3 #18 #19 #20 #21

Definition of Done
- Keep issue open until explicit non-prod live-AWS evidence is attached.

- Product credibility depends on a non-author operator reproducing value in real AWS.

Scope

  - auth/bootstrap
  - tenant/team setup
  - environment create/provision
  - job create/submit
  - run monitor/cancel
  - logs/diagnostics/cost view

Acceptance Criteria

- Evidence artifacts are linked (with operation_id/run_id/EMR JobRun IDs).
- Any blockers are documented with follow-up issues.

Related

Problem

Scope

title: [Dagster] Enforce strict client interface checks and dedupe config normalization

labels.name: status:needs-live-aws-evidence
labels.description: Do not close until explicit live-AWS proof is linked

body:
Problem
- _looks_like_sparkpilot_client currently uses any(...), allowing partial clients.
- Config normalization logic is duplicated between ops/assets, risking behavior drift.

Scope
- Require all required client methods (or Protocol-based validation).
- Move config normalization into one shared utility and reuse across ops/assets.
- Add tests for strict interface validation and shared normalization behavior.

Acceptance Criteria
- Partial client objects fail fast with clear diagnostics.
- Ops/assets share one normalization implementation.
- Tests cover interface validation and normalization parity.

References
- Parent issue: #46
- Review findings: Important (interface check) + Minor (duplication).

Definition of Done
- Link non-prod live-AWS evidence artifacts + runtime IDs and test/build results.

title: [Dagster] Map discovery/token HTTP failures into SparkPilot domain errors

labels.name: status:needs-live-aws-evidence
labels.description: Do not close until explicit live-AWS proof is linked

body:
Problem
- Token/discovery raise_for_status() can surface raw httpx.HTTPStatusError.
- Dagster ops retry/error logic expects SparkPilotTransientError / SparkPilotPermanentError.

Scope
- Wrap token/discovery HTTP failures into SparkPilot domain errors consistently.
- Classify transient vs permanent status failures for retry policy.
- Add tests for 401/403/404/429/5xx + network failure mapping.

Acceptance Criteria
- Dagster ops never leak raw HTTPStatusError for token/discovery paths.
- Retry behavior is deterministic and documented.
- Tests verify mapped exception classes and retry boundaries.

References
- Parent issue: #46
- Review finding: Important error-handling gap in Dagster client.

Definition of Done
- Link non-prod live-AWS evidence artifacts + runtime IDs and test/build results.

title: [Dagster] Narrow _compat fallback to import errors and fail fast otherwise

labels.name: status:needs-live-aws-evidence
labels.description: Do not close until explicit live-AWS proof is linked

body:
Problem
- providers/dagster/_compat.py catches broad Exception and silently falls back to compatibility stubs.
- Real Dagster runtime/import failures can be masked.

Scope
- Catch only ImportError/ModuleNotFoundError (or tightly scoped import-path errors).
- Fail fast for unexpected runtime exceptions during compatibility import.
- Add tests that prove unexpected exceptions are not swallowed.

Acceptance Criteria
- Only missing optional dependency paths use compatibility fallback.
- Runtime/import regressions surface as explicit failures.
- Tests cover both allowed fallback and fail-fast branches.

References
- Parent issue: #46
- Review finding: Critical reliability/architecture bug in Dagster integration.

Definition of Done
- Link non-prod live-AWS evidence artifacts + runtime IDs and test/build results.

title: [Security] Throttle JWKS forced refresh on invalid signatures

labels.name: status:needs-live-aws-evidence
labels.description: Do not close until explicit live-AWS proof is linked

body:
Problem
- Every InvalidSignatureError currently forces JWKS refresh.
- Malformed token floods can drive outbound JWKS refresh storms.

Scope
- Add minimum refresh interval / backoff for forced JWKS refresh.
- Restrict forced refresh to tighter conditions (e.g., known kid + stale cache window).
- Emit metrics/logging for forced refresh and throttle events.
- Add tests for repeated invalid signatures and refresh throttling behavior.

Acceptance Criteria
- Repeated bad signatures do not trigger unbounded JWKS fetches.
- Legitimate key rotation still recovers correctly.
- Telemetry clearly shows refresh attempts and throttling decisions.

References
- Parent issues: #15, #35
- Review finding: Important security/scalability risk in OIDC verifier.

Definition of Done
- Link non-prod live-AWS evidence artifacts + runtime IDs and test/build results.

title: [Auth] Allowlist external OIDC issuer/token origins in CSP connect-src

labels.name: status:needs-live-aws-evidence
labels.description: Do not close until explicit live-AWS proof is linked

body:
Problem
- CSP connect-src 'self' blocks browser requests to external OIDC discovery/token endpoints.

Scope
- Add explicit allowlist for OIDC issuer/discovery/token origins in CSP connect-src.
- Drive configuration from validated env vars.
- Keep deny-by-default behavior for unknown origins.
- Add tests for same-origin and external-issuer configurations.

Acceptance Criteria
- External OIDC login succeeds with allowlisted issuer origins.
- Non-allowlisted origins remain blocked.
- CSP config is documented with secure defaults.

References
- Parent issues: #35, #15
- Review finding: Important production readiness gap in OIDC flow.

Definition of Done
- Link non-prod live-AWS evidence artifacts + runtime IDs and test/build results.

title: [Security] Replace localStorage bearer token handling and remove unsafe-inline CSP

labels.name: status:needs-live-aws-evidence
labels.description: Do not close until explicit live-AWS proof is linked

body:
Problem
- Access tokens are persisted in localStorage while production CSP allows 'unsafe-inline'.
- Any XSS can exfiltrate bearer tokens.

Scope
- Remove browser token persistence (localStorage/sessionStorage) for auth credentials.
- Move to HttpOnly; Secure; SameSite cookie-backed session/proxy model.
- Remove 'unsafe-inline' in production CSP (nonce/hash-based script policy).
- Add regression tests for token handling and CSP headers.

Acceptance Criteria
- No bearer token is readable from browser JS storage APIs.
- Production CSP has no 'unsafe-inline' for scripts.
- Authenticated UI API flows still work end-to-end.
- Security-focused tests pass in CI.

References
- Parent issues: #35, #15
- Review finding: Critical token theft risk in UI auth/CSP.

Definition of Done
- Link non-prod live-AWS evidence artifacts + runtime IDs and test/build results.

title: [R03] Upgrade Cost Tracking to CUR-Aligned Chargeback

labels.name: status:needs-live-aws-evidence
labels.description: Do not close until explicit live-AWS proof is linked

body:
Roadmap ID: R03

## Problem Statement
Current run-cost estimate is not CUR-reconciled and not sufficient for enterprise chargeback.

## Why It Matters
FinOps teams require actual-vs-estimated cost attribution and budget enforcement.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Add CostAllocation model aligned to CUR line items
- [ ] Integrate CUR data via Athena queries
- [ ] Map namespace/virtual cluster to cost center
- [ ] Propagate team/project tags to EMR job runs and EC2 resources
- [ ] Add showback API endpoint for team/period breakdown
- [ ] Add TeamBudget model with warn/block thresholds
- [ ] Integrate budget checks into preflight
- [ ] Add CUR reconciliation worker
- [ ] Add unit tests for allocation/budgets/reconciliation
- [ ] Add integration test with real CUR data in S3

## Out of Scope
- Cross-cloud billing normalization

## Test Strategy
- Unit tests for cost and budget rules
- Integration test against Athena/CUR dataset

## Dependencies
- R18 (#31)

## Definition of Done
- [ ] Acceptance criteria complete.
- [ ] Evidence artifacts linked in an issue comment.
- [ ] Unit tests added/updated where applicable.
- [ ] Integration or real-AWS validation linked where required.
- [ ] Documentation updates linked where applicable.

title: Full BYOC 1G: Real-AWS full-BYOC validation matrix and reproducible smoke flow

labels.name: status:needs-live-aws-evidence
labels.description: Do not close until explicit live-AWS proof is linked

body:
Parent: #1

Goal
Execute and document full-BYOC real-AWS validation scenarios.

Interfaces
- validation docs under docs/validation/
- smoke scripts under scripts/smoke/ for full-BYOC happy + negative paths

Failure modes
- stage failures without actionable remediation
- non-deterministic retry causing duplicate infra
- cross-tenant contamination in tags/state

Test strategy
- happy path at least one region
- negative path per stage with reproducible commands
- resume/retry/cancel checks under worker restart

title: Full BYOC 1F: Security hardening and full-BYOC permission baseline

labels.name: status:needs-live-aws-evidence
labels.description: Do not close until explicit live-AWS proof is linked

body:
Parent: #1

Goal
Finalize full-BYOC role model and least-privilege policy baseline.

Interfaces
- policy artifacts for full-BYOC customer bootstrap
- full-BYOC preflight checks for required IAM actions and trust conditions

Failure modes
- missing iam:PassRole
- ExternalId mismatch in trust
- overbroad grants violating guardrails

Test strategy
- policy simulation/trust parsing tests
- preflight fail-fast remediation tests
- security checklist run before GA enablement

title: Full BYOC 1E: Failure classification, compensation, and cleanup workflow

labels.name: status:needs-live-aws-evidence
labels.description: Do not close until explicit live-AWS proof is linked

body:
Parent: #1

Goal
Harden partial-failure behavior and add controlled cleanup operations.

Interfaces
- ProvisioningErrorClassifier.classify(error) -> RetryDecision
- cleanup operation path (proposed endpoint: POST /v1/environments/{id}/cleanup)
- cleanup checkpoints with operation locking

Failure modes
- cleanup on untagged customer-owned resources
- provisioning/cleanup race
- partial cleanup with inconsistent metadata

Test strategy
- classifier unit tests
- integration tests for cleanup idempotency and locking
- transient failure injection during cleanup

title: Full BYOC 1D: Implement provisioning_eks and provisioning_emr stages

labels.name: status:needs-live-aws-evidence
labels.description: Do not close until explicit live-AWS proof is linked

body:
Parent: #1

Goal
Provision EKS and EMR virtual cluster stages with deterministic readiness checks.

Interfaces
- FullByocProvisioner.provision_eks(context) -> StageResult
- FullByocProvisioner.provision_emr(context) -> StageResult
- AWS client helpers for readiness polling, OIDC association verification, namespace/RBAC verification

Failure modes
- EKS create timeout
- missing OIDC association
- EMR VC create failures (namespace access, trust issues)

Test strategy
- unit tests for readiness/OIDC/trust checks
- integration tests for staged retry + idempotent re-entry
- real-AWS success + known-failure signature tests

title: Full BYOC 1C: Implement provisioning_network stage

labels.name: status:needs-live-aws-evidence
labels.description: Do not close until explicit live-AWS proof is linked

body:
Parent: #1

Goal
Provision VPC/subnet/network baseline for full BYOC with explicit ownership tagging.

Interfaces
- FullByocProvisioner.provision_network(context) -> StageResult
- checkpoint metadata includes created_resource_refs for network resources

Failure modes
- CIDR overlap / subnet create failures
- NAT or endpoint provisioning errors
- permission denied on network APIs

Test strategy
- unit tests for network inputs and tag ownership rules
- integration tests for retryable vs permanent classification
- real-AWS validation in disposable account

title: Full BYOC 1B: Terraform orchestrator and operation checkpointing

labels.name: status:needs-live-aws-evidence
labels.description: Do not close until explicit live-AWS proof is linked

body:
Parent: #1

Goal
Introduce an internal Terraform orchestration layer and persist resumable stage checkpoints.

Interfaces
- new module: src/sparkpilot/terraform_orchestrator.py
- new types: ProvisioningStageContext, TerraformPlanResult, TerraformApplyResult
- process_provisioning_once integration for full-BYOC staged execution

Failure modes
- Terraform binary/version mismatch
- backend state lock timeout
- non-zero plan/apply exit with structured diagnostics capture

Test strategy
- unit tests for command construction and parse
- integration tests for checkpoint persistence + resume
- API contract tests for operation state transitions

title: BYOC-Lite OIDC missing detection and remediation (detect+instruct default)

labels.name: status:needs-live-aws-evidence
labels.description: Do not close until explicit live-AWS proof is linked

body:
Scope
- Detect missing EKS OIDC provider and return remediation instructions by default.
- Do not mutate IAM/OIDC automatically in default mode.

Required permissions for optional automation mode
- eks:DescribeCluster
- iam:CreateOpenIDConnectProvider

Acceptance Criteria
- Missing OIDC provider is detected and reported with clear fix steps.
- Default behavior is detect+instruct (no IAM mutation).

Parent: #2
Depends on: #3

title: Full BYOC design doc and decision record (Issue #1A)

labels.name: status:needs-live-aws-evidence
labels.description: Do not close until explicit live-AWS proof is linked

body:
Goal
Produce the design doc required to unblock full BYOC implementation.

Deliverables
- Decision on provisioning orchestrator (Terraform/CloudFormation/CDK) with tradeoffs.
- State machine and persistence model for long-running infrastructure operations.
- Failure model and rollback behavior for partial failures across VPC/EKS/IAM/EMR.
- Security model for customer account boundaries and minimum role permissions.
- Proposed phased implementation plan and test strategy.

Acceptance Criteria
- Design doc reviewed by lead and approved.
- Follow-on implementation issues created from approved design.

title: Establish production deployment baseline for API/workers/database/observability

labels.name: status:needs-live-aws-evidence
labels.description: Do not close until explicit live-AWS proof is linked

body:
Problem
System currently runs locally only; no production deployment baseline exists.

Scope
- Define deployable architecture for API, scheduler/reconciler workers, and persistent database.
- Add secrets management, health checks, structured logging, and baseline monitoring.
- Provide deployment documentation and minimal runbooks.

Acceptance Criteria
- A non-local environment can run end-to-end reliably.
- Core service SLIs are observable.
- Recovery steps are documented for common failures.

title: Test and harden cancellation and retry behavior under transient AWS failures

labels.name: status:needs-live-aws-evidence
labels.description: Do not close until explicit live-AWS proof is linked

body:
Problem
Retry and cancellation paths have not been validated against real transient errors.

Scope
- Simulate or induce throttling/network/transient AWS failures in dispatch and reconcile paths.
- Verify retry policy behavior and terminal state correctness.
- Validate cancellation at queued, accepted, and running states.

Acceptance Criteria
- Retry behavior is deterministic and documented.
- Cancellation works from each lifecycle phase with correct final state.
- Automated tests cover transient failure branches.

title: Add support and validation for long-running Structured Streaming jobs

labels.name: status:needs-live-aws-evidence
labels.description: Do not close until explicit live-AWS proof is linked

body:
Problem
Only batch-style smoke jobs were validated; long-running streaming lifecycle is untested.

Scope
- Validate run lifecycle for streaming workloads (start, health, cancellation, restart semantics).
- Ensure heartbeat/status model supports long-lived runs.
- Provide one reference streaming workload for verification.

Acceptance Criteria
- Streaming run stays healthy over a sustained interval.
- Cancellation behaves deterministically and updates state.
- Logs and metrics remain accessible during long runtime.

title: Validate private EKS networking scenarios (private endpoint, private subnets, NAT)

labels.name: status:needs-live-aws-evidence
labels.description: Do not close until explicit live-AWS proof is linked

body:
Problem
Current validation does not cover private cluster endpoint or restricted network topologies.

Scope
- Test BYOC-Lite provisioning and run execution against private-endpoint EKS.
- Validate S3/CloudWatch/STS connectivity paths in private subnet deployments.
- Document networking prerequisites and failure modes.

Acceptance Criteria
- One successful private-network E2E run is documented.
- Required VPC endpoints/NAT assumptions are captured.
- Common network failures map to actionable error messages.

title: [R10] Karpenter Integration for Dynamic Spot Node Provisioning

labels.name: status:needs-live-aws-evidence
labels.description: Do not close until explicit live-AWS proof is linked

body:
Roadmap ID: R10

## Problem Statement
Spot strategy needs operational integration with Karpenter for dynamic, diversified capacity provisioning.

## Why It Matters
Without Karpenter-aware validation and guidance, Spot reliability and savings are inconsistent.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Preflight check validates Karpenter is installed on the target EKS cluster.
- [ ] Preflight check validates a Karpenter NodePool exists that supports Spot instances.
- [ ] Preflight check validates NodePool instance type diversification (3+ types across 2+ families).
- [ ] Optional: SparkPilot can create/manage a dedicated Karpenter NodePool for Spark workloads during environment provisioning.
- [ ] NodePool config includes Spot + on-demand fallback, Graviton preference, and consolidation policy.
- [ ] Documentation for recommended Karpenter NodePool config for EMR-on-EKS.
- [ ] Unit tests for Karpenter validation.
- [ ] Integration test with real Karpenter installation.

## Out of Scope
- Replacing Karpenter itself.
- Managing unrelated Kubernetes workloads.

## Test Strategy
- Unit tests for Karpenter install and NodePool validation paths.
- Integration test on Karpenter-enabled cluster.
- Real-AWS preflight evidence with remediation output.

## Dependencies
- R18 (#31)
- R01 (#32)

## Definition of Done
- [ ] Acceptance criteria complete.
- [ ] Evidence artifacts linked in an issue comment.
- [ ] Unit tests and integration evidence linked.
- [ ] Documentation updates linked.

title: UI: differentiate BYOC vs BYOC-Lite on environment detail and lists

labels.name: status:needs-live-aws-evidence
labels.description: Do not close until explicit live-AWS proof is linked

body:
Problem
UI does not clearly distinguish provisioning modes or show mode-specific infrastructure data.

Scope
- Display provisioning_mode in environment list and detail views.
- BYOC-Lite: show cluster ARN, namespace, virtual cluster ID, execution role.
- Full BYOC: show provisioning progress and infra outputs.

Acceptance Criteria
- Users can identify mode at a glance in UI.
- Mode-specific fields render correctly from API payloads.
- Empty-state handling is explicit when fields are unavailable.

title: Narrow run-logs ClientError handling to ResourceNotFound and surface other AWS errors

labels.name: status:needs-live-aws-evidence
labels.description: Do not close until explicit live-AWS proof is linked

body:
Problem
Current broad ClientError catch can mask real log-access and AWS failures.

Scope
- Only suppress expected CloudWatch ResourceNotFound behavior.
- Log and surface all other ClientError types.
- Add typed error mapping for common AWS failures.

Acceptance Criteria
- ResourceNotFound returns empty logs without 500.
- AccessDenied/throttling/service errors are visible in API responses and logs.
- Tests cover all mapped error branches.

title: Remove placeholder default for emr_execution_role_arn and require explicit configuration

labels.name: status:needs-live-aws-evidence
labels.description: Do not close until explicit live-AWS proof is linked

body:
Problem
A placeholder default execution role ARN caused silent misconfiguration and wasted debugging.

Scope
- Eliminate placeholder ARN defaults from runtime configuration.
- Require explicit configuration via environment variable, settings file, or environment record.
- Fail fast during startup or run submission if missing.

Acceptance Criteria
- Service cannot submit runs with placeholder or empty execution role ARN.
- Error message includes exact setting name and expected ARN format.
- Unit tests cover missing and invalid ARN paths.

title: Add preflight IAM/IRSA validation before StartJobRun

labels.name: status:needs-live-aws-evidence
labels.description: Do not close until explicit live-AWS proof is linked

body:
Problem
Runs failed with IAM/IRSA issues only after submission, increasing time-to-diagnosis.

Scope
- Validate customer role permissions, execution role trust policy, iam:PassRole, and IRSA/OIDC assumptions before dispatch.
- Expose preflight results and remediation details via API/CLI/UI.
- Gate scheduler dispatch on hard preflight failures.
- Add reconciler fallback diagnostics for active legacy runs created before preflight rollout.

Acceptance Criteria
- Invalid IAM/IRSA config blocks new run dispatch with actionable errors.
- Scheduler applies preflight before StartJobRun.
- Reconciler records diagnostic preflight result for legacy active runs lacking preflight evidence.

title: Full BYOC design spike: architecture, tool selection, and failure model

labels.name: status:needs-live-aws-evidence
labels.description: Do not close until explicit live-AWS proof is linked

body:
Problem
Full BYOC behavior is still simulated in code and has never been validated end-to-end on real AWS.

Scope (design spike only)
- Produce a design doc that selects orchestration approach (Terraform vs CloudFormation vs CDK).
- Define state management model for long-running provisioning and retries.
- Define partial-failure and rollback strategy across VPC, EKS, IAM, and EMR APIs.
- Define account-boundary and security assumptions for customer-owned infrastructure.

Out of Scope
- Implementing VPC/EKS provisioning adapters.
- Shipping runtime code for Full BYOC provisioning.

Acceptance Criteria
- Design doc is reviewed and approved.
- Implementation child issues are created only after the design decision is finalized.
- Child issues include explicit interfaces, failure modes, and test strategy.
